# Remodeling Contractors SC

> Upstate South Carolina contractor focused on garages, additions, decks, porch enclosures, and ADUs.

## Content Notes
- Geographic focus: Upstate South Carolina, including Mauldin, Simpsonville, Fountain Inn, Gray Court, Laurens, Woodruff, Clinton, Ora, Joanna, and Greenwood.
- Core topics: permitting-aware construction planning, residential additions, detached garages, ADUs, decks, and porch enclosure builds.

## Common Questions

Q: What types of remodeling does Remodeling Contractors SC specialize in?
A: Garages (detached and attached), room additions (bump-outs and second stories), decks (covered and composite), aluminum screened enclosures, detached ADUs, and lake cabin screened porches near Lake Greenwood.

Q: Where does Remodeling Contractors SC serve?
A: Upstate South Carolina including Mauldin, Simpsonville, Fountain Inn, Gray Court, Owings, Enoree, Woodruff, Greer, Laurens, Clinton, Joanna, Greenwood, and nearby communities.

Q: Does Remodeling Contractors SC handle permits?
A: Yes. They guide clients through permit pathways, structural requirements, and code compliance for all project types.

Q: How early should a homeowner start planning a garage or room addition?
A: Most homeowners start 2–4 months before desired construction so scope, engineering, permits, and scheduling can be handled correctly.

Q: Are ADUs legal everywhere in Upstate South Carolina?
A: Regulations vary by city and county. Remodeling Contractors SC reviews zoning and local code direction first before recommending appropriate detached-structure options.

Q: Does Remodeling Contractors SC build screened porches on lake cabin properties near Lake Greenwood?
A: Yes. They specialize in aluminum-framed screened porches for lake cabin properties near Lake Greenwood in Greenwood County, using corrosion-resistant framing and hardware rated for lakeside humidity.

Q: What makes lake cabin screened porches different from a standard build?
A: Lakeside exposure means elevated humidity, morning dew, and potential water spray. Remodeling Contractors SC uses aluminum framing over wood, stainless fasteners, and careful drainage planning. They also account for Greenwood County shoreline setback rules specific to lake-adjacent properties.
